{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 65th Cannes Film Festival | image = 2012 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 65th Cannes Film Festival featuring a photo of American actress [[Marilyn Monroe]] | opening = ''[[Moonrise Kingdom]]'' | closing = ''[[Thérèse Desqueyroux (2012 film)|Thérèse Desqueyroux]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Amour (2012 film)|Amour]]'' | host = [[Bérénice Bejo]] | number = 22 (In Competition) | date = 16 – 27 May 2012 | website = {{URL|www.festival-cannes.com}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2011 Cannes Film Festival|2011]] | next = [[2013 Cannes Film Festival|2013]] }} The '''65th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 16 to 27 May 2012.{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en.html |title=Festival de Cannes 2012 |access-date=11 December 2011 |work=festival-cannes.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151016162039/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/archives/2012/allSelections.html |archive-date=16 October 2015 }} Italian filmmaker [[Nanni Moretti]] was the president of the jury for the main competition.{{cite news |url=http://latimesblogs.latimes.com/movies/2012/01/cannes-jury-president-nanni-moretti.html |title=Nanni Moretti named 2012 Cannes jury president |access-date=21 January 2012|newspaper=LA Times|date=20 January 2012 }} Austrian filmmaker [[Michael Haneke]] won the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[Amour (2012 film)|Amour]].'' The official poster of the festival features [[Marilyn Monroe]], to mark the 50th anniversary of her death.{{cite web |date=28 February 2012 |title=The iconic actress and sex symbol was selected in tribute to the 50th anniversary of her death. |url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/cannes-poster-marilyn-monroe-film-festival-295738 |access-date=3 March 2012 |work=hollywoodreporter.com}} French actress [[Bérénice Bejo]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite web |title=''The Artist'' star to host Cannes film ceremonies |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/cannes-film-festival/9211616/The-Artist-star-to-host-Cannes-film-ceremonies.html |access-date=19 April 2012 |work=The Telegraph}} The festival opened with ''[[Moonrise Kingdom]]'' by [[Wes Anderson]], and the closed with ''[[Thérèse Desqueyroux (2012 film)|Thérèse Desqueyroux]]'' by [[Claude Miller]].{{Cite web|date=18 April 2012 |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/58882.html |title=Claude Miller's last film to close the Festival de Cannes |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120805002846/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/58882.html |archive-date=5 August 2012 }} [[File:Jury Cannes 2012.jpg|thumb|272x272px|The main competition jury; from left to right: Alexander Payne, Andrea Arnold, Jean Paul Gaultier, Hiam Abbass, Emmanuelle Devos, Raoul Peck, Diane Kruger, Nanni Moretti, Ewan McGregor, and festival president Gilles Jacob]] ==Juries== ===Μain competition=== [[File:Nanni Moretti Cannes 2012 2.jpg|thumb|237x237px|Nanni Moretti, President of the main competition jury]] The following people were appointed as the Jury for the feature films of the 2012 Official Selection:{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2012/allJury.html |title=All Juries 2012 |work=festival-cannes.fr filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/58948.html |title=The Jury of the 65th Festival de Cannes |work=festival-cannes.com |publisher=[[Cannes Film Festival]] |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120524235524/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/58948.html |archive-date=24 May 2012 }} * [[Hiam Abbass]], Palestinian juries awarded films in the frame of the Critics' Week. '''Nespresso Grand Prize''' * [[Bertrand Bonello]], French filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web |url=http://www.semainedelacritique.com/films/2012/2012_jury.php |title=Feature Films Jury |access-date=28 April 2015 |work=Semaine de la Critique |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305044600/http://www.semainedelacritique.com/films/2012/2012_jury.php |archive-date=5 March 2016 }} * Francisco Ferreira, Portuguese film critic * Akiko Kobari, Japanese film and dance critic * Robert Koehler, American film critic * Hanns-Georg Rodek, German film critic '''France 4 Visionary Award''' * [[Céline Sciamma]], French filmmaker - Jury President * Victor-Emmanuel Boinem, Belgian film student and blogger * Ryan Lattanzio, American student and lead film critic at [[The Daily Californian]] * Bikas Mishra, Indian founder and editor of [[DearCinema.com]] * Kim Seehe, South Korean student and film critic '''Nikon Discovery Award for Short Film''' * [[João Pedro Rodrigues]], Portuguese film director - Jury President * Jakub Felcman, Czech film curator * [[Marianne Khoury]], Egyptian film director and producer * Danny Lennon, Canadian film curator * [[Kleber Mendonça Filho]], Brazilian film director, curator, and critic ==Official Selection== The official selection was announced on 19 April at Grand Hôtel in Paris. Among comments after the announcement, journalists noted the unusually high number of Hollywood films in the line-up, the absence of any female director in the main competition, as well as the absence of competing first-time feature film directors.{{Cite web |last=Bradshaw |first=Peter|author-link=Peter Bradshaw |date=19 April 2012 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/filmblog/2012/apr/19/cannes-film-festival-2012-lineup |title=Cannes film festival 2012 lineup: the competition's still a man's world |work=guardian.co.uk |publisher=The Guardian|access-date=20 April 2012}}{{Cite web |last=Leffler |first=Rebecca |date=19 April 2012 |url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/cannes-film-festival-thierry-fremaux-qa-314227 |title=Cannes 2012: Thierry Fremaux on Increased Star Power and the Festival's Mission (Q&A) |work=The Hollywood Reporter |access-date=20 April 2012}} The festival's artistic leader Thierry Frémaux responded that people should not focus only on the competition films: "The selection is an ensemble; you have to consider the whole package." ===In Competition=== The following feature films competed The feature competition consists entirely of directorial debuts, something the section's artistic director Charles Tesson stressed was not intentional, but only the way it turned out when the submissions had been judged by quality. Cops]]'' |[[Quentin Dupieux]] |France |} :'''(CdO)''' indicates film eligible for the Caméra d'Or as directorial debut feature. ==Official Awards== [[File:Michael Haneke 2009.jpg|thumb|210px|[[Michael Haneke]], winner of the 2012 Palme d'Or]] [[File:Matteo Garrone cropped.jpg|thumb|210px|[[Matteo Garrone]], winner of the 2012 Grand Prix]] The [[Palme d'Or]] was won by the French-language film ''[[Amour (2012 film)|Amour]]'' directed by [[Michael Haneke]]. Haneke previously won the award for ''[[The White Ribbon]]'' in [[2009 Cannes Film Festival|2009]].{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/cannes-film-festival/9293702/Cannes-2012-Michael-Hanekes-Love-wins-Palme-dOr.html|title=Cannes 2012: Michael Haneke's 'Love' wins Palme d'Or|date=27 May 2012|work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=27 May 2012}} ''Love'' tells the story of an elderly couple preparing for death.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/filmblog/2012/may/27/cannes-2012-michael-haneke-amour?newsfeed=true|title=Cannes 2012: Amour's love is justified, but why cold-shoulder Cosmopolis? |last=Brooks |first=Xan |date=27 May 2012 |work=The Guardian |access-date=27 May 2012}} During his acceptance speech, the director said "A very, very big thanks to my actors who have made this film. It's their film. They are the essence of this film." Moretti said that none of the winners had been selected unanimously, and described such an outcome as "a middle ground that would have pleased no one". He revealed that ''Holy Motors'', ''Paradise: Love'' and ''Post Tenebras Lux'' were the entries that most had divided the jury.{{Cite web |url=http://www.cineuropa.org/2011/nw.aspx?t=newsdetail&l=en&did=221697 |title=Nanni Moretti: 'No prize was agreed on unanimously' |last=Lemercier |first=Fabien |date=28 May 2012 |work=cineuropa.org |publisher=Cineuropa |access-date=28 May 2012}} The following films and people received the 2012 Official selection awards:{{Cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2012/allAward.html |title=Awards 2012: All the awards |work=festival-cannes.com |publisher=Cannes Film Festival |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170207060814/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2012/allAward.html |archive-date=7 February 2017 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2012.htm |title=65ème Festival de Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |access-date=29 July 2017}} === In Competition === * [[Palme d'Or]]: ''[[Amour
